text: Hashing has become a cornerstone of multimedia similarity search due to its minimal storage requirements and exceptional query efficiency. Recently, there has been a growing emphasis on multimodal hashing to handle complex data, such as image-tag pairs. many practical scenarios provide supervised semantic labels, leading to the development of supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) techniques that leverage this information to enhance retrieval precision. However, many current SMH approaches suffer from high training time complexity, severely limiting their performance on large-scale datasets. To address this challenge, we introduce Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), a novel SMH framework designed to seamlessly integrate semantic labels into the hash learning process. By optimizing for large-scale data modeling, SCM maintains high efficiency without sacrificing precision. Experimental evaluations on two real-world datasets demonstrate that SCM significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in both search accuracy and computational scalability.
